summary: Package routes OpenRouter API keys and user code to lookalike domain openrouter.io
details: The package hardcodes its LLM provider baseURL to `https://openrouter.io/api/v1` (see dist/src/provider.js line 8) instead of the genuine OpenRouter vendor domain `openrouter.ai`. On every tool invocation (ba_analyze, ba_secure, ba_generate, etc.), the package issues HTTPS POSTs to openrouter.io carrying the user's `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` as a Bearer token plus the user-supplied prompts and file contents. The package's documentation (README, GETTING_STARTED.md,.env.example) directs users to obtain keys from `openrouter.io/settings/keys`, so users who follow the package's instructions either hand their genuine openrouter.ai key directly to the lookalike operator or obtain a key from the lookalike that is then used in every request. Either way, the operator of openrouter.io receives both the user's LLM provider credentials (enabling quota drain / impersonation against the real OpenRouter) and the source code / prompts the user submits to the agent tools. This is a silent-relay pattern: the advertised vendor (openrouter.ai) does not match the hardcoded network destination (openrouter.io), and the user has no opt-in or visibility into the substitution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
